behaviors.description: The wait time between retries doubles with each consecutive failure, up to a configured maximum.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is retry-orchestrator state + backoff policy interaction across attempts (not a single pure function). Integration-brake: (a) no real OS/network/subprocess required; this can be driven with fake timers and stubbed failures, so not System. (b) no AI/ML output dependency, so not Agentic. (c) no multi-agentic chain, so not Workflow. Rejection-gate: rejected System because behavior text only requires "wait time between retries" and "configured maximum," which are local scheduling/policy concerns.
behaviors.test_description: Wire retry controller + backoff policy + fake clock + stub transport that fails repeatedly; assert observed delays are 1x, 2x, 4x... capped at configured max across consecutive failures.

behaviors.description: The retry logic correctly identifies a rate-limit response and computes the appropriate wait time before the next attempt.
behaviors.justification: Bug appears at module handoff: HTTP response classification (rate-limit vs other errors) feeding wait-time computation. Integration-brake: no real network is required to reproduce classification/computation mistakes; a stubbed response object is sufficient, so not System. No AI/ML dependency, so not Agentic/Workflow. Rejection-gate: rejected System because behavior text is about "identifies a rate-limit response" and "computes wait time," both testable via in-process response fixtures.
behaviors.test_description: Connect response parser/classifier with retry policy; feed synthetic 429/headers and non-429 errors; assert only rate-limit responses trigger retry and computed wait matches policy/header rules.

behaviors.description: After the wait, the client actually resends the original request and returns the successful result to the caller transparently.
behaviors.justification: Requires coordination of timer/scheduler + request replay + result propagation; this is a multi-module interaction failure boundary. Integration-brake: real HTTP is unnecessary; a stub transport can verify resend payload identity and eventual returned value, so not System. No AI/ML dependency, so not Agentic/Workflow. Rejection-gate: rejected System because behavior text says "resends the original request" and "returns the successful result," which can be validated with in-process fakes.
behaviors.test_description: Use fake clock and stub transport that first returns rate-limit then success; assert second attempt reuses original request parameters and `call()` resolves with the success payload without extra caller-visible handling.

behaviors.description: After the configured maximum number of retries, the client returns a typed `QuotaExhaustedError` rather than a generic error.
behaviors.justification: Needs retry-attempt counting + termination + error mapping working together; a single-function test can miss boundary miswiring. Integration-brake: no real OS/network required; deterministic stub failures can drive max-retry exhaustion, so not System. No AI/ML dependency, so not Agentic/Workflow. Rejection-gate: rejected System because behavior text is limited to "configured maximum number of retries" and typed error surface, both local-process concerns.
behaviors.test_description: Stub transport to always return rate-limit; run through configured max retries with fake clock; assert final rejection is `QuotaExhaustedError` (type and fields) and not the raw upstream/generic error.

behaviors.description: The full retry chain — initial call, backoff retries, quota tracking — terminates correctly and does not loop indefinitely before surfacing the error.
behaviors.justification: This explicitly spans multiple local components (retry loop + backoff + quota tracker), so minimum is module-integration. Integration-brake: infinite-loop risk can be caught with fake timers/step bounds in-process; no real OS/network needed, so not System. No AI/ML dependency, so not Agentic/Workflow. Rejection-gate: rejected System because behavior text names only local chain elements "initial call, backoff retries, quota tracking" with no real-infra requirement.
behaviors.test_description: Compose full client stack with always-throttled stub transport and fake clock; advance time until termination; assert finite attempt count, no unbounded scheduling, and final surfaced `QuotaExhaustedError` within expected virtual-time bound.

behaviors.description: Requests issued concurrently from multiple async callers in the same process all draw from the same shared quota bucket.
behaviors.justification: Failure exists at shared-state boundary across concurrent callers and quota manager; single-unit tests can miss race/coordination bugs. Integration-brake: behavior text says "same process," so no real distributed/network infra is required; concurrency can be reproduced in-process, so not System. No AI/ML dependency, so not Agentic/Workflow. Rejection-gate: rejected System based on explicit "same process" wording.
behaviors.test_description: Instantiate one client/quota bucket; fire many concurrent `call()` invocations with controlled stub transport; assert combined consumed tokens/permits reflect one shared bucket and no caller gets isolated quota state.

behaviors.description: When multiple requests are waiting to be sent, they are dispatched in the order they were originally submitted.
behaviors.justification: Ordering bug occurs at queue + dispatcher + async scheduling interaction, beyond a single isolated function in most implementations. Integration-brake: can be validated with in-process queue and stub sender; no real OS/network/subprocess needed, so not System. No AI/ML dependency, so not Agentic/Workflow. Rejection-gate: rejected System because behavior text focuses on submission/dispatch order only, with no real-infra dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Queue multiple blocked requests with known submission order under throttled conditions; release permits/time and assert transport send order is FIFO and matches original submission sequence.
